capture_method.description: Native (non-WASM) execution of the real OverhangClassifierDefault::run_finalization via slicer_sdk::test_prelude, the same harness used by modules/core-modules/overhang-classifier-default/tests/basic_tdd.rs. No WASM dispatch, no repo files modified. Driven by a scratch cargo binary (path dependency on the workspace member crate) built and run outside the repo tree.

scenario.name: overhang-ramp-then-ceiling
scenario.description: 6 global layers (index 0..5), one OuterWall square entity (entity_id=1) per layer, wall width=0.4. Layer 0 is an unclassified support base (10x10 square). Layers 1-3 progressively offset outward from the previous layer's footprint (offsets -0.05, -0.35, -0.95 mm per side) to produce increasing overhang severity. Layer 4 steps far inward (a 2x2 square fully inside layer 3's footprint) to produce a fully-supported (non-overhanging) entity. Layer 5 repeats layer 4's exact footprint (sd == 0 boundary case).

config_case_A_defaults.FACT: With all overhang_*_4_speed config keys at their manifest default (0.0), run_finalization takes the early-return branch (lib.rs lines 74-76: `if oh1==0.0 && oh2==0.0 && oh3==0.0 && oh4==0.0 { return Ok(()); }`) and emits NO SetSpeedFactor mutations at all, regardless of geometry. Verified against the scenario above: mutation_count == 0.
config_case_A_defaults.entity_speed_factor_digest_note: No digest recorded - there is nothing to digest; zero entities are touched in any configuration when all four overhang speeds are 0.0 (this is a config-driven no-op, not scenario-dependent).

config_case_B_configured.factor_to_quartile_mapping_note: factor = overhang_speed_for_quartile(q) / base_speed_for_role(OuterWall=60.0). By construction: quartile 1 -> factor 0.8, quartile 2 -> factor 0.6, quartile 3 -> factor 0.4, quartile 4 -> overhang_4_4_speed is configured (12.0, factor 0.2) but is NEVER applied because run_finalization (lib.rs line 81) unconditionally skips any entity whose classified quartile is >= 4.

quartile_bucket_counts.q4_note: no factor applied; entity retains implicit factor 1.0 (untouched)

config_case_B_configured.entity_speed_factor_digest_canonical_lis:
layer=1 entity_id=1 quartile=3 factor=0.4
layer=2 entity_id=1 quartile=1 factor=0.8
layer=3 entity_id=1 quartile=1 factor=0.8
layer=4 entity_id=1 quartile=4 factor=none
layer=5 entity_id=1 quartile=3 factor=0.4

notes: This baseline exercises the module's own signed-distance-to-previous-layer-wall-geometry classifier (classify.rs classify_layers / LinesDistancer2D), which is entirely independent from the newer PrePass::OverhangAnnotation mesh-cross-section pipeline introduced in packet 106 (ADR-0022). This module is a PostPass::LayerFinalization-stage consumer, wired via WIT world slicer:world-finalization@1.0.0, reading/writing LayerCollectionIR only - it never reads SurfaceClassificationIR.overhang_quartile_polygons in its current (pre-refactor) form.

Quartile 4 (fully supported, sd > 0) is a structural no-op in the current implementation: run_finalization always skips it (lib.rs line 81), so overhang_4_4_speed is dead configuration in this version - any post-refactor implementation that DOES emit a mutation for quartile 4 is a documented behavior change, not a regression, and should be called out explicitly against this baseline.
Boundary case sd == 0 (layer 5, identical footprint to layer 4) classifies as quartile 3, not quartile 4, per classify.rs's strict `sd > 0.0` test for quartile 4.
